title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market activity for May 23, 2026, and weekend outlook

sections.title: Market Pulse: Bullish Momentum Meets Consumer Jitters as Fed Swears in New Chair

items.headline: Wall Street Continues Upward Trend, S&P 500 Achieves Eighth Straight Winning Week
items.summary:
U.S. equity markets closed higher on Friday, May 23, extending a period of broad market strength. The S&P 500 marked its eighth consecutive weekly gain, the longest unbroken run since December 2023, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average reached a new record high. This bullish sentiment is largely driven by strong corporate earnings, enthusiasm around artificial intelligence, and signs of progress in US-Iran peace negotiations.

However, a growing divergence between Wall Street and Main Street is evident, as the University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index fell to a record low of 44.8 in May 2026, marking a third straight monthly decline. Consumers are increasingly worried about inflation, which they forecast to worsen in the coming 12 months, and high oil prices due to the ongoing Iran war.

Kevin Warsh was sworn in as the 17th Federal Reserve Chair on Friday, succeeding Jerome Powell. The Fed's latest Financial Stability Report highlighted geopolitical risks and AI financing as key concerns. Market participants are anticipating the Fed to hold rates steady for now, with some officials indicating potential rate hikes if inflation persists. (Not financial advice)

items.headline: Ross Stores Surges on Strong Earnings
items.summary: The off-price retailer reported profit and revenue for the latest quarter that easily cleared analysts' expectations, boosting its stock.

items.headline: Estee Lauder Jumps After Dropping Merger Talks
items.summary: Estee Lauder jumped after announcing it was no longer considering a possible merger with Puig, the Spanish fragrance and beauty products company.

items.headline: Zoom Communications Rises on Strong Profit Report
items.summary: Zoom Communications delivered a better profit report for the latest quarter than analysts expected.

items.headline: ARM Holdings Sees Historic Weekly Gain
items.summary: ARM Holdings ripped nearly 50% in a single week, contributing to the strong performance of the AI and chip sectors.

items.headline: Nvidia Pulls Back Amid Mixed Trading Activity
items.summary: Despite a blockbuster earnings report, Nvidia dropped amidst mixed trading activity, possibly due to valuation concerns.

items.headline: Walmart Declines on Cautious Sentiment and Guidance Miss
items.summary: Walmart's stock declined, reflecting cautious sentiment and being hit hard by a Thursday guidance miss.

sections.title: Market News: Key Stories Affecting Investors

items.headline: Sector Spotlight: Tech and Energy Lead Year-to-Date; Healthcare and Cyclicals Gain Momentum
items.summary:
The energy sector remains the biggest winner year-to-date, up over 30%, with technology stocks making a strong comeback, up 22.3% year-to-date and 10.6% in May alone. Healthcare was the strongest sector in the S&P 500 on Friday, rising 1.19%, posting its best week in six months.

Investors are also rotating into economically sensitive groups like Real Estate, Consumer Cyclical, and Small and Microcap stocks. Conversely, Communication Services was the only sector to finish in negative territory on Friday, declining 0.54%, partly due to weakness in Alphabet. The utilities sector has been a significant loser in May, sliding 4.9%, potentially due to concerns about the impact of AI data centers on electric grids.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Sworn In as New Fed Chair Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Kevin Warsh officially became the 17th Chairman of the Federal Reserve on Friday, May 23. His tenure begins at a challenging time, with recent economic data showing persistent inflation. Former Fed Chair Jerome Powell, in his final FOMC meeting, offered a reality check on inflation, stating it has not yet peaked due to the Iran war-driven energy supply shock. Fed Governor Christopher Waller has also indicated that rate hikes are possible if inflation does not abate soon.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Hits Record Low as Inflation Expectations Rise
items.summary: The University of Michigan's consumer sentiment index dropped to a record low of 44.8 in May 2026, marking the third consecutive monthly decline. This fall is largely attributed to high prices and growing expectations among U.S. consumers that inflation will worsen. They are forecasting inflation to reach 4.8% in the next 12 months, up from 4.7% last month. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) and Producer Price Index (PPI) inflation figures are both at 3+ year highs.

items.headline: Treasury Yields Climb Amidst Economic Headwinds
items.summary: Yields on Treasury bonds rose sharply this week, with the 30-year note climbing over 5%, reaching its highest level in 19 years. The 10-year Treasury yield edged down to 4.56% on Friday but remains elevated. Rising borrowing costs could impact mortgage rates for consumers and potentially curtail corporate borrowing for initiatives like AI data centers, which have recently supported economic growth.

items.headline: Strong Earnings Reports Continue to Buoy Market Optimism
items.summary: The ongoing earnings season continues to deliver strong results, with several companies surpassing analyst expectations. Ross Stores, Workday ($WDAY), and Zoom Communications ($ZM) are recent examples that reported better-than-expected profits. This cavalcade of strong corporate earnings has helped U.S. stocks remain near their record highs, reflecting the resilience of corporate profits despite broader economic uncertainties.
